summary: Read(fileID) resolves via cs.cat.Get first (catalog-as-source-of-truth), wraps ErrNotFound for never-created fileIDs, then os.ReadFile's ContentPath(fileID) verbatim with no trimming/transcoding. TestContentRead is a genuine bytes.Equal byte-for-byte assertion; TestContentReadNotFound uses a fresh fileID (999) never passed to Create and asserts errors.Is(err, ErrNotFound) plus nil data. Diff is purely additive (+79/-0 across content.go and content_test.go); no changes to Create/OpenContentStore/ContentPath. go build, go vet, and go test ./catalog/... -race -v -count=1 all pass, including the pre-existing 1.4.1 tests (TestContentCreate, TestContentCreateInvalidFileID) with zero regressions.

requirements_conformance.evidence: Acceptance criterion ('reading a file by fileID returns its current full markdown content exactly as last written') is met: Read reads the same ContentPath(fileID) Create wrote via writeContentFile's atomic tmp+rename, no encoding/trimming applied. Test spec's exact command (go test ./engine/catalog/... -run TestContentRead) passes with a real bytes.Equal comparison, not length/substring.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/catalog.md states catalog is 'the source of truth for a file's identity... and the anchor point' other modules coordinate through; Read's cs.cat.Get-first ordering matches this and mirrors Create's existing convention. Pre-MVCC single-version (v1-only) framing is consistent with LLD's currentVersion field being reserved for mvcc.md's future CAS-based versioning; Read does not read rec.CurrentVersion at all, so 1.4.3 (append) can introduce version-aware path resolution without needing to rework Read's control flow, only its ContentPath resolution -- no append-incompatible assumption found (e.g. no full-buffer size caching, no offset assumptions).

regression_risk.evidence: Diff is additive only (new Read method + 2 new tests); Create, OpenContentStore, ContentPath, writeContentFile unmodified. Full engine/catalog test suite (incl. TestContentCreate, TestContentCreateInvalidFileID, catalog/file-manager/allocator/record-encode suites) passes under -race with zero failures.

edge_cases.evidence: Empty-file content (zero-byte Create then Read) is not explicitly tested, though os.ReadFile on a zero-byte file correctly returns ([]byte{}, nil) so behavior is almost certainly correct -- low risk, but an explicit test would remove doubt. The claimed 'catalog record exists but content file missing/unreadable' distinct-error path (e.g. a catalog Put succeeded but the content file was deleted out-of-band, or a manually corrupted catalog record pointing at a wrong/nonexistent path) is described in the Read doc comment and code (the second os.ReadFile error branch) but is NOT covered by any test in content_test.go -- this is a real coverage gap relative to the implementer's own claimed design, though not required by subtask 1.4.2's literal acceptance criteria/test spec. Read-immediately-after-Create-in-same-process is implicitly covered by TestContentRead itself (no separate process/cache staleness risk since ContentStore does no in-memory content caching).

security.evidence: Error messages (fmt.Errorf with fileID and cs.ContentPath(fileID)) do include the absolute/relative file path in the second error branch, but this matches the existing convention already used by Create's writeContentFile and catalog.go's Get/Delete (internal, non-network-facing engine package) -- no new information-disclosure surface introduced beyond what 1.4.1 already exposed. No injection/traversal risk: fileID is a uint64, not attacker-controlled string, so ContentPath cannot be manipulated to escape cs.dir.

performance.evidence: Read does one catalog lookup (striped-mutex, O(1)) plus one os.ReadFile syscall -- no unnecessary copies or lock contention beyond what Create already established. No N+1 or unbounded-loop concerns for a single-file read path.

maintainability.evidence: Doc comments clearly explain the pre-MVCC v1-only scope and the two distinct error modes (not-found vs. record-exists-but-file-missing), matching the surrounding file's documentation density and style. Symmetry with Create's error-wrapping conventions (catalog: content read: ... : %w) keeps grep-ability for error provenance.

test_coverage.evidence: TestContentRead and TestContentReadNotFound satisfy the literal test spec and are genuine (bytes.Equal, errors.Is with a fresh unused fileID 999). Gaps: (1) no test for the claimed 'catalog exists, content file missing/corrupted' distinct-error branch despite it being explicitly designed and documented in the Read function; (2) no empty-content-round-trip test; (3) no test asserting Read's error is NOT ErrNotFound in the missing-content-file case (i.e. nothing pins down that the two error paths stay actually distinguishable via errors.Is, only that ErrNotFound works in the not-found case).

confidence.evidence: High confidence in core acceptance-criteria correctness (byte-for-byte read-back, not-found handling) given clean diff, passing race-enabled full suite, and direct code inspection. Confidence is only 'PASS_WITH_COMMENTS' rather than 'PASS' because the second error branch (record exists / file missing) -- part of the implementer's own claimed design -- is entirely untested, so its behavior is verified by code reading only, not by an executed test.

possible_improvements.evidence: Add TestContentReadMissingContentFile (Create a record via cat.Put only, or os.Remove the content file after a normal Create, then assert Read returns a non-nil, non-ErrNotFound error) and TestContentReadEmptyContent (Create with []byte{} or nil, then Read, assert zero-length byte-for-byte match) to close the two coverage gaps noted above before 1.4.3 builds on this path.

build_and_test.go_test_race: pass (56.899s, engine/catalog package, all subtests including TestContentCreate, TestContentCreateInvalidFileID, TestContentRead, TestContentReadNotFound)
